text: Protein supplementation during chronic endurance training increases aerobic capacity (VO2peak) by approximately 0.89 mL/kg/min compared to endurance training alone.
Protein supplementation during endurance training stimulates greater lean mass gain of approximately 0.32 kg compared to no supplementation.
Protein supplementation during endurance training improves time trial performance by approximately 29 seconds compared to control.
Protein supplementation provides an additional 26.4% gain in VO2peak beyond the improvements attributed to endurance training alone.

Protein supplementation combined with endurance training produces a small-to-moderate positive effect on combined aerobic capacity measures (VO2peak and peak workload power) with a standardized mean difference of 0.36.
Endurance training alone produces substantial improvements in VO2peak of approximately 3.67 mL/kg/min, with protein supplementation adding a further ~1 mL/kg/min on top of that.
specific_value: 3.67 mL/kg/min from training + 0.97 mL/kg/min from protein
